option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
# Example application.properties for SpringAIQuickStart # This file is safe to commit to Git. It contains placeholders instead of secrets.
spring.application.name=SpringAIQuickStart
server.port=8080
# DeepSeek configuration (example placeholders) # Copy this file to src/main/resources/application.properties and fill in the real values locally. spring.ai.deepseek.base-url=https://api.deepseek.com/ # DO NOT commit your real API key. Replace the value below in your local application.properties spring.ai.deepseek.api-key=YOUR_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY_HERE spring.ai.deepseek.chat.options.model=deepseek-chat # model options spring.ai.deepseek.chat.options.temperature=0.8
# Notes: # 1) Add "src/main/resources/application.properties" to your .gitignore to avoid committing secrets. # 2) You can also use environment variables or external config mechanisms for the API key in production. # For example, set SPRING_AI_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY in your environment and reference it via ${SPRING_AI_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY}. # 3) To use this template locally, run: # copy src\main\resources\application.properties.example src\main\resources\application.properties # (Then edit the copied file and put your real API key.)
